_comment: Bug report deck (filed → triaged → reproduced → root-caused → fixed → verified) for a real CSS regression in the Agent Actions drawer (AgentActionRow.vue .aar__title): a flex child without min-width:0 can't ellipsis-truncate, so long agent-action titles overflow their card. before/after rrweb clips are captured deterministically by tools/runstatus/tests/playwright/slidey-128-clipbug-rrweb-capture.spec.ts (real .aar* CSS + the real slidey-128 run's agent-action titles, bug on / off). The fix (min-width:0) is committed in AgentActionRow.vue. meta.mode:pitch is required or scenes render blank.

scenes.title: Agent Action titles overflow their card
scenes.subtitle: Long titles spill past the drawer and collide with the token counts

scenes.title: The filed issue

messages.author: devon
messages.body: The Agent Actions drawer looks broken when an agent emits a long reasoning or result title — the title runs straight off the right of its card and sits on top of the token and cost numbers. Reproduces every time on verbose THINK / RESULT rows.

messages.author: maintainer
messages.body:
Confirmed. `.aar__title` is a flex child with no `min-width: 0`, so `text-overflow: ellipsis` never fires and the title overflows.

Severity: P2 — cosmetic, but the row is unreadable.
Area: runstatus · Agent Actions drawer.

scenes.caption: Filed by a developer, triaged to a missing CSS rule — area and severity assigned.
scenes.narration: Filed by a developer — the drawer is unreadable on long titles. Triage confirms a missing CSS rule, with severity and area.

scenes.title: Steps to reproduce

cards.label: Open a session with agent actions
cards.sub: any kitsoki web run that emits a transcript

cards.label: Find a long THINK or RESULT row
cards.sub: a title past about sixty characters

scenes.caption: Deterministic — every long-titled row exhibits it.
scenes.narration: To reproduce: open the Agent Actions drawer on any session, and find a reasoning or result row with a long title.

scenes.title: Expected vs actual

right.label: Actual
lines: Title runs off the right edge

scenes.caption: The title should clip; instead it overflows.
scenes.narration: Expected: the title truncates inside its card. Actual: it runs off the edge and overlaps the cost figures.

scenes.title: The bug in the real drawer
scenes.caption: Each long reasoning / result title runs straight off the right edge, over the in/out token counts and cost.
scenes.narration: Here it is in the real UI — long titles spill straight past their card.

scenes.title: AgentActionRow.vue · .aar__title (pre-fix)

annotations.text: A flex child — its implicit minimum is its content width
So a long title can't shrink and this never engages → it overflows

scenes.caption: The classic flexbox trap: text-overflow on a flex child does nothing without min-width: 0.
scenes.narration: The root cause: the title is a flex child with no minimum width, so the ellipsis never engages.

scenes.title: Resolution

items.label: Fix
items.detail: Add min-width: 0 to .aar__title so the flex child can shrink and the ellipsis engages. One line, no markup change.

items.label: Regression
items.detail: A capture test asserts the long titles overflow before and fit after.

scenes.caption: A one-line fix, locked in by a deterministic geometry test.
scenes.narration: The fix is one line — min-width zero on the title — committed and covered by a deterministic geometry test.

scenes.title: After the fix
scenes.caption: Every title truncates inside its card and the token / cost cluster sits where it belongs.
scenes.narration: After the fix, every title truncates inside its card and the columns line up again.
